title: corythophora rimosa subsp. rubra
intro: Corythophora rimosa subsp. rubra, also known as red coral tree, is a plant species that belongs to the family Thymelaeaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its anthraquinone glycosides, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The health benefits associated with Corythophora rimosa subsp. rubra include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a sought-after ingredient in herbal remedies. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
